active_projects.description: Add deviation tracking fields to core artifact formats: handoff-artifact.md (new Deviations from Plan section), summary-format.md (new Plan Deviations section), progress-file.md (deviations array in schema), context-exhaustion-detection.md (final checkpoint protocol), and plan-format-enforcement.md (deviation annotation requirements). These format changes define the contract that tasks 569 and 570 implement.

artifacts.summary: Research report documenting current state of 5 artifact format files and specific proposed changes for deviation tracking support
4-phase plan to update 5 artifact format files with deviation tracking fields, checklist annotation format, and final checkpoint protocol
Implementation summary for task 568: updated 5 artifact format files with deviation tracking fields

active_projects.completion_summary: Added deviation tracking schema (deviations array), annotation syntax (*(deviation: skipped|altered|deferred — ...)*), and new sections (## Deviations from Plan in handoffs, ## Plan Deviations in summaries) across five core artifact format files, establishing the format contract for tasks 569 and 570.

active_projects.description: Enhance general-implementation-agent.md with: (1) Post-phase self-review step (new Stage 4D-ii) — after marking a phase [COMPLETED], re-read the phase subtask list and verify each objective was addressed, noting any skipped items; (2) Progressive handoff updates — update the handoff document incrementally as phases complete, not only at context exhaustion; (3) Deviation annotation in plan — when deviating from plan, annotate the checklist item with what was done differently and why; (4) Final context-exhaustion checkpoint — when detecting imminent exhaustion, update both progress file AND plan file as last actions before writing the handoff.

artifacts.summary: Research report with exact insertion points and wording for 4 improvements to general-implementation-agent.md

Implementation summary: added 4D-ii post-phase self-review, 4D-iii progressive handoff, 4B-ii deviation annotation Step 4, 4E Step 1.5 final checkpoint, and updated Stage 6 template + Phase Checkpoint Protocol

active_projects.completion_summary: Enhanced general-implementation-agent.md with post-phase self-review (4D-ii), progressive handoffs (4D-iii), deviation annotation during execution (4B-ii Step 4), final plan annotation checkpoint (4E Step 1.5), updated summary template with Plan Deviations section, and Phase Checkpoint Protocol update.

active_projects.description: Propagate the post-phase self-review, deviation tracking, progressive handoff updates, and final context checkpoint improvements to all extension implementation agents: nix-implementation-agent, neovim-implementation-agent, lean-implementation-agent, and any others (python, web, z3, latex, typst if present). These agents currently lack most of general-implementation-agent.md's progress tracking, self-review, handoff, and deviation features. Bring each to parity using the updated formats from task 568 and patterns from task 569.

active_projects.description: Investigate and implement context: fork + agent: frontmatter for core delegating skills to enable CLAUDE_CODE_FORK_SUBAGENT=1 cache sharing, following corrected understanding of fork mechanics from official Claude Code docs

artifacts.summary: Revised plan to investigate and implement context: fork + agent: frontmatter for core delegating skills, enabling FORK_SUBAGENT cache sharing with 4 phases: audit, pilot, rollout, documentation

Definitive architecture analysis: hybrid inline-agent approach has no precedent, context: fork + orchestration IS proven (slide skills), cache sharing is fundamentally incompatible with named agent routing, recommend rescoping task 500 to documentation-only

memory_candidates.content: All loaded extension skills (neovim-research, neovim-implementation, nix-research, nix-implementation) use the same Pattern A delegation as core skills -- explicit Task tool with subagent_type and multi-stage postflight. They do NOT use context: fork or agent: frontmatter. Only unloaded extension skills (e.g., slide-planning, slide-critic) use Pattern B with context: fork.

The present extension's skill-slide-planning and skill-slide-critic skills use context: fork + agent: in frontmatter AND explicit subagent_type in their Task call. This proves that context: fork with multi-stage orchestration (preflight, interactive Q&A, delegation, postflight) works correctly.
Claude Code agent definitions support a skills field that preloads skill content into the subagent context at startup. This is described as the inverse of context: fork.

active_projects.description: Optimize team-mode skills (team-research, team-plan, team-implement) to maximize FORK_SUBAGENT parallel cache sharing benefits for teammates 2-N

artifacts.summary: Team-mode skills are already eligible for FORK_SUBAGENT cache sharing; optimization focuses on prompt restructuring, dynamic token multiplier calculation, and cache-aware metadata reporting.
4-phase plan to restructure team-mode prompts for FORK_SUBAGENT cache sharing, replace hardcoded token multiplier with dynamic formula, and update documentation

memory_candidates.content: TeammateTool spawns in team-mode skills do NOT specify subagent_type, making them eligible for CLAUDE_CODE_FORK_SUBAGENT=1 cache sharing. Teammates 2-N inherit the parent's prompt cache prefix, reducing input token cost by ~90%. Both CLAUDE_CODE_EXPERIMENTAL_AGENT_TEAMS=1 and CLAUDE_CODE_FORK_SUBAGENT=1 must be enabled. The actual cache benefit depends on the byte-identical shared prefix ratio -- prompts should put shared content first and teammate-specific assignments last to maximize overlap.

skill-team-research hardcodes team_size=4 in Stage 1 regardless of user-provided --team-size flag. The four fixed roles are: Primary (A), Alternatives (B), Critic (C), Horizons (D). The token_usage_multiplier is hardcoded at 5.0 in all team skill metadata but should be dynamically calculated.
